items.markdown:
**iPaaS** stands for **Integration Platform as a Service** . It is a suite of cloud-hosted tools that connects disparate software applications, systems, and data sources so they can communicate with one another.[](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/ipaas) [[1]](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/ipaas)[[2]](https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/ipaas/)[[3]](https://www.oneio.cloud/blog/ipaas-solutions-and-vendors-compared)

Why is it used?

As organizations adopt more Software as a Service (SaaS) applications, data often gets trapped in isolated "silos." iPaaS solves this by acting as a centralized cloud hub that synchronizes data and automates workflows between these apps, legacy systems, and on-premises databases.[](https://www.workato.com/the-connector/ipaas-complete-guide/) [[1]](https://www.workato.com/the-connector/ipaas-complete-guide/)[[2]](https://frends.com/ipaas/blog/what-is-ipaas-integration-platform-as-a-service-explained)

Core Capabilities

- **Pre-built Connectors:** Ready-made integrations for popular tools (e.g., Salesforce, Workday, Shopify) that eliminate the need to build code from scratch.[](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/ipaas) [[1]](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/ipaas)[[2]](https://www.workato.com/the-connector/ipaas-complete-guide/)
- **Low-Code/No-Code Builders:** Visual interfaces that allow non-technical business users ("citizen developers") to easily map out data flows.[](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/ipaas) [[1]](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/ipaas)[[2]](https://www.appseconnect.com/what-does-ipaas-stand-for/)[[3]](https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/ipaas/)[[4]](https://www.appseconnect.com/13-best-ipaas-solutions/)
- **API Management:** Tools to design, publish, and secure application programming interfaces.[](https://www.workato.com/the-connector/ipaas-complete-guide/) [[1]](https://www.workato.com/the-connector/ipaas-complete-guide/)
- **Data Transformation:** Translates different data formats so different systems can understand each other.[](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/ipaas) [[1]](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/ipaas)[[2]](https://squadra.nl/en/insights/exploring-strategies-for-seamless-integration-ipaas-esb-and-api/)[[3]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HE5uyPu_HIs)[[4]](https://www.workato.com/the-connector/ipaas-complete-guide/)

Common Use Cases

- **Employee Onboarding:** Automatically pushing HR and payroll data from a platform like Workday into IT, finance, and email systems.
- **Customer Syncing:** Ensuring real-time data flow between a marketing tool (e.g., Mailchimp) and a CRM (e.g., Salesforce).[[1]](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/ipaas-examples)[[2]](https://www.workato.com/the-connector/ipaas-complete-guide/)

Popular Platforms You can explore industry-leading solutions via these platforms:

- Workato Automation Platform
- [IBM Cloud Integration](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/ipaas) 
- [AWS Cloud Integration](https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/ipaas/) 
- [SAP Integration Platform](https://www.sap.com/africa/resources/what-is-ipaas)

items.title: What is meant by iPaaS?

expanded_element.title: What is iPaaS? - Integration Platform as a Service Explained
expanded_element.description: Integration platform as a service (iPaaS) is a cloud-based software model for integrating data from multiple applications into a single solution. Most organizations today have multiple applications that handle various aspects of their business processes and IT operations.

items.title: What is the difference between PaaS and iPaaS?

expanded_element.title: PaaS vs IPaaS: Differences and Advantages - Boomi
expanded_element.description:
iPaaS vs PaaS
 While both iPaaS and PaaS serve their own unique purposes, iPaaS emphasizes data flow and system connectivity,whereas PaaS centers on software development and application lifecycle management. Depending on your tech eco-system, you can choose which model works best for you.
